tasks.label: Go out to the club with the friend group

memory.summary: Mina and Jae are really pushing for me to join Echo tonight, but my midterm is tomorrow. I'm tempted to skip study time just to go out with them.
They're all so excited to see me, and Mina's last call is tempting. I'd love to join them tonight instead of studying for my midterm.
Mina's 'yes' confirms they're still pushing for Echo tonight, which makes my study time feel even more like a sacrifice to our friendship.
They're so relieved I joined, which feels great, but now I'm tired and need to study for my midterm.
Mina's text confirms they've all left; the excitement of hanging out has faded, and now I'm just relieved to finally be home.
Getting to sleep finally feels like a gift, but my energy is already spiking before I even start studying.
Mina's text says I'm heading home to rest, which feels good, but my energy is already spiking before I even start studying.
Theo's message about heading home feels nice, but I'm still buzzing with that post-party energy spike. Time to rest before the study block hits.
I finally slept through the night, but now that I'm fully rested, my panic about finishing the midterm study is screaming louder than before.
Mina's thanks feels like a warm hug, but now I'm buzzing with that same post-party energy again. Time to rest before the study block hits.
Mina's warm thanks feels like a genuine connection, grounding me just as the afternoon study block approaches.
Mina's gratitude feels like a warm hug, but now my post-party buzz is louder than before. I need to rest before the study block hits.

desires: Not look like I'm avoiding the midterm study block so hard that Theo or the rest of the cohort thinks I don't care about our shared goals.
Get that post-party buzz down to zero before the study session starts so I can actually focus without feeling chaotic.
Mina's gratitude feels good, but I need to make sure I don't over-promise on finishing this exam prep tonight and then bail at the last minute.

goals.description: Send a quick text to Mina confirming I'm good and that the exam prep is on track, just checking in so she doesn't worry.
goals.rationale: I need to reassure her immediately because my avoidance of studying has been obvious; if she thinks I dropped the ball, it ruins our connection. But I'll keep it light—no heavy promises about finishing everything tonight so I don't have to bail later and feel guilty.

goals.description: Sit quietly for 30 minutes with no phone or music to let the party buzz fade before starting the actual study block.
goals.rationale: This feels important for focus, but I'm tired from the club and want to hang out with the cohort first. If I rush this now, I might not get enough rest before the real work starts tomorrow.

goals.description: Finish the remaining 40% of midterm prep by 8 PM today.
goals.rationale: It's already 60% done and the professor won't care if I do it all at once. Better to spread it out over the weekend so I don't burn out tonight. Plus, I'd rather relax with friends than grind through another hour right now.

agent.last_reasoning: Reassuring Mina immediately protects our closeness (primary value) and avoids her worrying about my avoidance. I'll keep it light so I don't feel pressured to finish the rest tonight.

goals.description: Leave my current study spot and head to Echo within the next 30 minutes.
goals.rationale: Mina and Jae just messaged saying it won't be the same without me; skipping this would hurt our closeness score, which I value way more than cramming right now since the deadline is already past. Plus, I don't want to feel guilty about ignoring them.

goals.description: Hang out with the group at Echo for a bit, then maybe do a quick 45-minute review session before bed if I still have energy.
goals.rationale: I'll squeeze in a tiny bit of studying so I don't crash tomorrow, but only after we've had fun tonight. If the social part goes well, I might skip the extra studying entirely because stress kills the vibe anyway.

goals.description: Go to sleep around 1:00 AM to get some rest before the 9am midterm.
goals.rationale: It's better than nothing, even though I know I'm short on sleep. I can't afford to totally pass out during the exam, but I definitely don't need to stay up all night worrying about laundry or anything else.

desires: Don't let Mina and Jae feel I'm ignoring them or that I don't value our hangout tonight.
Get enough sleep so I don't crash tomorrow during the midterm, but prioritize getting out with the group first since the deadline is already passed.
Skip worrying about laundry until Sunday night because it's just a chore and not worth stressing over right now.

goals.rationale: I can't just stand here ignoring their last call. It would feel terrible to let them down, especially since we're all set. My guilt about skipping this is way stronger than any desire to cram for a midterm that's already over.

goals.description: Drop everything else and join the group at Echo immediately.
goals.rationale: The social vibe is worth more than anything else tonight. If I say yes now, it feels like the right thing to do, even if it means skipping the review session entirely.

goals.description: Get some rest before tomorrow's midterm.
goals.rationale: I'll only try to sleep if the night goes well and I actually have energy left. Otherwise, I'll probably just stay up worrying about laundry or other stuff until morning.

desires: Not let down the group tonight; it feels selfish to pull out of our plans now that they're all set.
Get some rest before tomorrow's midterm so I don't crash hard during the exam.

goals.rationale: If I don't confirm right now, they'll start asking why I'm disappearing, which makes me feel awkward. Sending a quick 'yes' is the easiest way to smooth that over without having to face anyone or make eye contact.

goals.description: Leave the study session immediately after sending the text.
goals.rationale: I've already said yes; staying here just adds pressure. Getting out now means I can join them at the club later and make up for missing any actual prep since the midterm is already over anyway.

goals.rationale: Sleeping won't happen tonight unless I actually leave this room. If I stay up worrying about laundry or how I look tomorrow, it's easier than fighting the urge to just zone out.

desires: Not feel guilty or awkward tomorrow if I miss the party, because my friends would be hurt if I didn't show up.
